title: RFC 9309 Robots Semantics and Library Conformance Adapter · Type-System Trace: Robots Status Policy
summary: The crawler fetches robots from the wrong origin, trusts a library without conformance fixtures, applies first-match rules, and treats outages as permission. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts robots status policy increment in RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser. Learners progress through prediction,…

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Classify successful, redirecting, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, and stale robots responses conservatively.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Robots Status Policy model · Theory Robots Statu…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Robots Status Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Robots Status Policy model · Theory Robots Statu…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 2113 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove RFC 9309 product, group, encoding, longest-match, response-state, cache, and library adapter behavior; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify successful, redirecting, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, and stale robots responses conservatively.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Robots Status Policy model · Theory Robots Statu…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Every robots retrieval failure means unrestricted crawling.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-robots-status-policy-104i8e(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_robots_status_policy_104i8e\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?robotsFixtures < 15)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?conformantFixtures < 15 \|\| conformantFixtures > robotsFixtures)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?failureCases < 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsafeAllows > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
